title: parartocarpus bracteatus
intro: Parartocarpus bracteatus, also known as the Indian almond, is a plant species native to Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible seeds. Traditionally, the seeds and leaves of Parartocarpus bracteatus have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Parartocarpus bracteatus is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its seeds are also used in traditional cooking and as a source of oil.
